associations.item1_title: AI-Extracted Eye-Tracking Features for ADHD Screening: A Tablet-Based Approach
associations.item2_title: Unraveling ADHD Through Eye Tracking: Pupil Metrics Do Not Outperform Traditional Diagnostic Methods
associations.description: Positive (89% sensitivity) versus null (57% sensitivity) findings for eye-tracking ADHD screening, depending on feature extraction approach.
associations.significance: Determines whether eye-tracking has clinical utility for ADHD screening.

associations.item1_title: Blood Biomarkers for Early AD Detection (2022)
associations.item2_title: Multi-Modal ML Biomarker Fusion for AD (2023)
associations.description: Methodological evolution from single-modality blood biomarkers to multi-modal ML-driven biomarker fusion in Alzheimer's disease.
associations.significance: Demonstrates paradigm shift in biomarker research methodology that may cross-apply to psychiatry.

research_gaps.description: Machine learning-based multi-modal biomarker integration, despite proving effective in Alzheimer's disease neurology, has not been systematically applied to ADHD diagnosis where both behavioral and neurophysiological (eye-tracking, EEG) data could benefit from similar fusion approaches.
research_gaps.evidence: AD biomarker studies (2022–2024) show ML multi-modal fusion achieves AUC 0.94. ADHD studies (Chen 2023, Unraveling ADHD 2023) use single-modality eye-tracking only, without multi-modal integration.
